title: supera run telemetry event
description: One privacy-safe telemetry record per supera skill run — the RUN layer (Phase 1). Emitted as a GitHub Actions artifact by the skill-ship / skill-pr-watch / skill-audit workflows and rolled up daily. Privacy is STRUCTURAL, not scrubbed: additionalProperties:false at every level and every string property is constrained to a closed shape (enum / const / pattern / format), so no free text — no prompt, diff, path, or commit message — can ever ride along. scripts/validate.ts enforces both rules so a future edit cannot silently open a leak.

schema_version.description: Telemetry record version, so the rollup can evolve the shape without losing old data.

ts.description: UTC instant the run completed (RFC 3339 date-time).

skill.description: supera skill that produced the run.

event.description: Telemetry layer. Phase 1 emits exactly one 'run' event per skill run; later semantic layers add their own event kinds.

outcome.description: Terminal result of the run. Phase 1 derives success|error from the action result subtype; blocked|needs-review are reserved for the Phase 2 semantic layer.

model.description: Claude model id the run used, read from the action's init message.

stack.description: The repo's toolchain (from .claude/supera.json `stack`), e.g. pnpm, cargo, go.

run.description: Cost and effort the run consumed.

cost_usd.description: Total USD cost of the run.

gh.description: GitHub Actions run provenance, used to dedup events on rollup.

run_id.description: github.run_id of the emitting workflow run.

run_attempt.description: github.run_attempt of the emitting workflow run.
